note: SMTP is configured entirely through the smtp.settings config object and a single settings form; there is no plugin type to implement and no drush command set, so all cases are `recipe` (response-graded), not `execution` (live-state).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the SMTP Authentication Support module installed, I want to route all outgoing mail through smtp.example.com on port 587 with a username and password. Which config keys in the smtp.settings config object do I set to turn SMTP on and point it at that server, and how do I authenticate? Name the keys.
evals.expected_output: In smtp.settings set smtp_on: true (master switch), smtp_host: 'smtp.example.com', smtp_port: '587', and authenticate with smtp_username / smtp_password.

evals.prompt: Where in the Drupal admin UI do I configure the SMTP Authentication Support module? Give the admin path.
evals.expected_output: Admin -> Configuration -> System -> SMTP Authentication Support at /admin/config/system/smtp (route smtp.config).

evals.prompt: How does the SMTP module actually deliver Drupal's mail through an SMTP server? What kind of Drupal plugin does it provide and what underlying library does it use?
evals.expected_output: It provides a core Mail plugin, Drupal\smtp\Plugin\Mail\SMTPMailSystem, that wraps the bundled phpmailer/phpmailer library; when smtp_on is true it becomes the active mail backend.

evals.prompt: I need to encrypt the SMTP transport with TLS on a Drupal site using the SMTP module. Which smtp.settings config key selects the encryption method, and what are its accepted values?
evals.expected_output: smtp_protocol selects the encryption; accepted values are standard, ssl, or tls (set it to tls). smtp_autotls can additionally upgrade the connection opportunistically.

evals.prompt: After configuring the SMTP module I want to verify mail actually goes out before trusting it. What built-in feature does the module offer, and which config key holds the destination for it?
evals.expected_output: The settings form has a send-a-test-email button that sends to the address in smtp_test_address; connectivity is checked by the ConnectionTester service.

evals.prompt: Which Drupal permission does the SMTP Authentication Support module define to let a role reach and change its settings?
On a Drupal staging site I want every outgoing email redirected to a single QA inbox so real users are never mailed. Which smtp.settings config key does the SMTP module provide for this?
evals.expected_output: smtp_reroute_address: when set, the SMTP plugin forces every message's recipient to that address (staging safety).

evals.prompt: I only want SMTP used for some of my Drupal site's mail, not every message. How do I do that with the SMTP module rather than enabling it globally?
evals.expected_output: Install drupal/mailsystem and assign the SMTP mail plugin to specific modules/keys there, instead of turning smtp_on on globally.
